---
title: Errors
description: Error types thrown by @justwant/cache.
icon: CircleAlert
---

## Error types

```ts
import { CacheError, CacheAdapterError } from "@justwant/cache";
```

| Class | When thrown |
|-------|-------------|
| `CacheError` | Base class for all cache errors |
| `CacheAdapterError` | The underlying adapter threw an error |

## CacheAdapterError

Thrown when an adapter operation fails (e.g. Redis connection lost). Wraps the original error:

```ts
import { CacheAdapterError } from "@justwant/cache";

try {
  await cache.get("key");
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof CacheAdapterError) {
    console.error("Adapter failed:", err.cause);
  }
}
```

## Suppress errors

To avoid throwing on adapter failures, set `onError` at cache creation time:

```ts
const cache = createCache({
  adapter: redisAdapter({ redis: redisClient }),
  onError: "silent", // Return null instead of throwing
});
```

See [Basic Usage](/docs/cache/basic-usage) for all `onError` values.
